title: tetraclinis articulata
intro: Tetraclinis articulata, also known as the Atlas cedar or Cedar of Atlas, is a coniferous evergreen tree native to the Atlas Mountains in North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and terpenes. The health benefits of Tetraclinis articulata include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and alleviate pain, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, Tetraclinis articulata is often used in teas, essential oils, and tinctures, as well as in aromatherapy and topical applications.
